Notes: S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. No strong radio-loud/jet-dominated X-ray evidence found; treated as radio-quiet.

S24 slab comptt result. S24 added this non-jetted nearby AGN; Eddington ratio uses Ark 564-specific BH mass and bolometric correction rather than the standard BASS entry. Super-Eddington catalog value; keep caveat.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. Included in later BASS radio-quiet/FGR-type selections; treated as radio-quiet.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. Treated as radio-quiet Seyfert.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. Super-Eddington value is driven by the very low catalog BH mass; use sensitivity tests.
S24 slab comptt result. S24 added this non-jetted nearby AGN. Multiple NuSTAR epochs were jointly fitted with kTe and tau tied; not an epoch-resolved value.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. Coronal parameters have large uncertainties; use low statistical weight or sensitivity tests.
S24 slab comptt result for the 2012 NuSTAR observation. Same observing epoch as Brenneman+2014 compTT/compPS work; use S24 for the homogeneous comptt table.

S24 slab comptt result using 2012-2015 NuSTAR data, including the 2012/2013 Balokovic+2015 observations. kTe and tau tied across epochs; not directly comparable to 2022 compps as pure time evolution. Classified as a Seyfert 1.9 in the source-specific NuSTAR/XMM studies; S24 lists it more generically, so the type column is standardized to Sy1.9.

S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale; super-Eddington catalog value and large kTe upper uncertainty require caveat. Treated as radio-quiet but with weak outflow/jet or compact-mm-emission discussion.
S24 slab comptt result. Traditionally radio-quiet, but recent VLBI work reports episodic relativistic jet activity; mark as radio-quiet with jet caveat.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. No strong radio-loud flag; treated as radio-quiet.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. Radio-quiet Seyfert 2; note later gamma-ray discussions when considering high-energy contamination.
S24 slab comptt result for the 2014 NuSTAR observation. Extreme high-temperature, very-low-depth solution near NuSTAR high-temperature sensitivity limits; use caveat.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. High optical depth and very low Eddington ratio; useful sensitivity-test point.
S24 slab comptt result. Eddington ratio from S24/BASS-Koss scale. Treated as radio-quiet; note possible high-energy/gamma-ray caveat in some discussions.
S24 slab comptt result for the 2012 NuSTAR campaign. Multiple NuSTAR observations were jointly fitted with kTe and tau tied; not split into individual epochs.

S24 slab comptt result. Included in the main sample as a borderline low-mass Seyfert/bare AGN. NuSTAR hard X-rays are likely AGN-dominated, but the source was flagged as possible SFG in a later BASS/Fermi selection; perform sensitivity tests.

Added non-S24 comptt entry. Adopt March 2014 simultaneous XMM-Newton+NuSTAR CompTT slab result; February 2013 had only a kTe lower limit and is excluded. Eddington ratio is epoch-matched Porquet+2019 value. Ark 120 is a bare/unabsorbed Seyfert 1, which is why the special "bare AGN" descriptor is kept in the notes rather than in the type column.

Added non-S24 COMPTT slab entry from 2016-2017 NuSTAR monitoring plus Swift-XRT. Changing-look AGN in a persistent Compton-thin state; campaign-level result, not five independent epochs. Eddington ratio is non-simultaneous Amorim+2024 catalog value. ESO 323-G77 is a changing-look AGN (CLAGN); this special classification is kept in the notes rather than in the type column.
